title: Delta Labs raises EUR 4.4 million for AI customer simulation platform
article_text: Delta Labs raises EUR 4.4 million for AI customer simulation platform 02.04.2026 Delta Labs has raised EUR 4.4 million in a seed round led by Cusp Capital and Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund. The company develops AI-powered customer simulation tools that help businesses anticipate how customers will respond to decisions before they are made. Most companies rely on surveys, focus groups and interviews to understand their customers. However, customers may be unreliable narrators of their own preference, which means the research that informs product, pricing and messaging decisions is distorted. Therefore, applying AI to accelerate those same inputs, as many tools now do, does not fix the underlying problem. Rather than generating responses from AI personas trained on generic internet data, Delta Lab’s first AI-tool Elaiia, which launched six months ago, builds a probabilistic model of a company's actual customer distribution, enriched with their own first-party signals. This allows teams to simulate how customers are likely to respond across product, pricing, messaging and strategy decisions ahead of time. Delta Labs was founded in 2022 by Anne Scherer, a former University of Zurich professor, and Cindy Candrian, who completed her PhD there. Their work draws on combined expertise in market research, quantitative modelling and human-technology interaction. The company had been bootstrapped until this funding round. The EUR 4.4 million seed round was backed by Cusp Capital and the Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund. Cusp Capital is an Essen, Germany-based venture capital firm focused on early-stage European software and technology companies, while the Auxxo Female Catalyst Fund is a Berlin-based early-stage fund established in 2019 that co-invests in European startups with at least one female founder holding a minimum of 20% of the founding shares. (AJ) Photo: Delta Labs Team. Photo courtesy of Delta Labs. 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Delta Labs AG Related tags

title: UBS makes a strategic investment in Artificialy
article_text: UBS makes a strategic investment in Artificialy 02.04.2026 Lugano-based Artificialy announced an a strategic investment from UBS, strengthening the partnership between Artificialy and the global banking group. Alongside the investment, UBS plans to significantly expand its collaboration with Artificialy through an enhanced commercial relationship, working to further scale and embed AI solutions across the firm. Artificialy supports organizations at every stage of their AI maturity level, from strategic AI transformation consulting and opportunity mapping, to co-developing advanced AI solutions as long-term partners, to delivering ready-to-use tools like its PrivateAI Suite. The company was founded in 2020 and focusses on AI solutions in production environments. The investment underscores UBS’s commitment to advancing artificial intelligence as a core capability across the firm and builds on an already close partnership between the two organizations. Daniele Magazzeni, Group Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer at UBS, said: “At UBS, we see AI as a key driver of our growth strategy and are committed to deploying it at scale to deliver meaningful benefits for our clients, employees and stakeholders. Our investment in Artificialy builds on a strong partnership and reflects our conviction in the importance of responsible and innovative AI. By working closely with Artificialy, we look forward to exploring new opportunities to apply AI solutions within our organization, supporting Switzerland’s growing AI ecosystem and continuing to deliver innovative, client-focused solutions.” Alberto Delorenzi, Chairman of Artificialy, commented: “This is a significant milestone for Artificialy. Welcoming a shareholder of UBS’s calibre sends a clear signal of confidence in our strategy and growth trajectory. This partnership gives us the backing to pursue our ambitions at a new scale – in Switzerland and beyond.” Luca Gambardella, Co-Founder and CEO of Artificialy, added: “UBS is not only investing in our company – they are also one of our most valued clients. That combination of capital and operational collaboration will help us ground our growth in real world deployment and measurable impact. We look forward to continuing to deliver AI solutions for UBS.” (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Artificialy SA Company profiles on startup.ch Artificialy SA Related News Artificialy SA 17.10.2025 09:00 New leadership drives growth in biotech and AI startups 05.06.2025 16:30 Abacus investiert in Tessiner KI-Consulting-Startup Related tags

title: Cohaga sammelt Millionenbetrag und lanciert Tool für Präsenz in KI-Antworten
article_text: Cohaga sammelt Millionenbetrag und lanciert Tool für Präsenz in KI-Antworten 02.04.2026 Die St. Galler cohaga AG betreibt die führende Schweizer Direktmarketing Plattform LeadHub. Das 2020 gestartete Unternehmen ist bootstrapped auf 30 Personen gewachsenen. Nun wurde erstmal eine Finanzierungsrunde durchgeführt. Gleichzeitig wurde ein neues Tool lanciert, das Unternehmen so positioniert, dass sie von KI nicht nur erkannt, sondern aktiv empfohlen und zitiert werden. Mitte März hat die cohaga AG eine Kapitalerhöhung durchgeführt. Zwei erfahrene Unternehmer und Business Angels aus dem Tech- und SaaS-Bereich beteiligen sich jetzt mit einem siebenstelligen Betrag am Unternehmen. «Diese Kapitalerhöhung ist für uns weit mehr als eine reine Finanzierung – sie ist ein klares Signal für das Vertrauen in unsere Vision und den bisherigen Weg. Wir haben bewusst lange unabhängig agiert und schaffen jetzt die Voraussetzungen, um unsere Entwicklung gezielt beschleunigen zu können.» sagt Isabel Bischof, Mitgründerin und COO der cohaga AG. Das zusätzliche Kapital ermöglichte es dem Team von COHAGA, in den vergangenen Monaten intensiv an einem neuen Produkt zu arbeiten, welches das Potential hat, den Marketing-Bereich nachhaltig zu verändern. «Während sich der cohaga LeadHub um Outbound-Leads kümmert, wird das neue Produkt mit dem Namen ranQ unseren Kunden planbar Inbound-Leads einbringen», ist Fabio Mätzler, Mitgründer und CEO überzeugt. Das neue Produkt hilft Unternehmen vom grundlegenden Wandel der Internetsuche von klassischen Suchmaschinen hin zu direkt generierten Antworten durch Systeme wie ChatGPT zu profitieren. Im Zentrum steht nicht mehr die Platzierung in Suchresultaten, sondern die direkte Integration in Antworten – dort, wo Nutzer heute Entscheidungen treffen. ranQ basiert auf dem Ansatz der sogenannten Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), einer Weiterentwicklung der klassischen Suchmaschinenoptimierung für KI-Systeme. Ziel ist es, Unternehmen so zu positionieren, dass sie von KI nicht nur erkannt, sondern aktiv empfohlen und zitiert werden. Damit greift ranQ in einen Markt ein, der sich aktuell grundlegend neu formiert: Sichtbarkeit entsteht nicht mehr über Rankings, sondern über Relevanz innerhalb von KI-Antworten. Im neuen Wettbewerbsumfeld gewinnt nicht mehr die lauteste, sondern die relevanteste Information. Wer es schafft, von KI-Systemen als vertrauenswürdige Quelle eingeordnet zu werden, sichert sich einen nachhaltigen Vorteil. Noch befindet sich dieser Markt in einer frühen Phase, doch die Dynamik ist hoch und die Entwicklung nicht mehr umkehrbar. «Die zunehmende Nutzung von KI verändert Kaufentscheidungen nachhaltig. Immer mehr Nutzer verlassen sich auf KI-generierte Empfehlungen, die gezielter und vertrauenswürdiger wahrgenommen werden. Unternehmen, die frühzeitig in dieser neuen Form der Sichtbarkeit investieren, sichern sich entscheidende Wettbewerbsvorteile», sagt Fabio Mätzler, CEO der cohaga AG. (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING LAUNCH More news about cohaga AG Company profiles on startup.ch cohaga AG Related News cohaga AG 11.01.2024 17:00 Adressdaten-Spezialist COHAGA auf Wachstumskurs Related tags

title: Covalo sichert sich Finanzierung in Millionenhöhe für nächste Wachstumsphase
article_text: Covalo sichert sich Finanzierung in Millionenhöhe für nächste Wachstumsphase 02.04.2026 Das Zürcher Startup Covalo hat in einer Seed-Erweiterungsrunde 3,5 Millionen Euro eingesammelt, angeführt von Hi inov und mit Beteiligung bestehender Investoren. Mit dem frischen Kapital will das Unternehmen seine Plattform für Inhaltsstoff- und Produktdaten ausbauen und KI-gestützte Innovationen weltweit vorantreiben. Im Bereich Körperpflege hängt Innovation stark von Inhaltsstoffdaten ab – doch diese sind über Unternehmen, Systeme und Formate fragmentiert. Ein Produkt kann bis zu 40 Rohstoffe mit jeweils rund 20 zugehörigen Dokumenten enthalten. Diese liegen in unterschiedlichen Strukturen vor, stammen von verschiedenen Lieferanten und nutzen unterschiedliche Nomenklaturen, was Teams in F&E, Regulierung und Einkauf viel Zeit für Datenerfassung, -aktualisierung und Compliance kostet. Gleichzeitig erschwert diese Fragmentierung den effektiven Einsatz von KI und modernen digitalen Workflows. Um den Aufwand zu reduzieren und Prozesse zu beschleunigen, hat das 2021 gegründete Startup Covalo eine Plattform entwickelt, die den Austausch von Inhaltsstoffdaten zentral und strukturiert abbildet. Sie ist direkt in die Systeme von Lieferanten sowie in F&E- und PLM-Prozesse der Marken integriert und ersetzt damit E-Mails, PDFs und Excel-Listen, die bislang den Datenaustausch dominieren. Aktualisiert ein Lieferant beispielsweise ein Compliance-Zertifikat, sehen alle Marken, die diesen Rohstoff nutzen, die Änderung. Wird ein neuer Rohstoff qualifiziert, fliessen die Daten direkt in Formulierungs- und Beschaffungsprozesse ein. Timo von Bargen, Mitgründer und CEO von Covalo erläutert: “Die Branche braucht ein gemeinsames Referenzsystem, in dem Inhaltsstoffdaten einmal strukturiert, einmal verwaltet und überall wiederverwendet werden. Unsere grössten Kunden erweitern ihre Nutzung der Plattform Jahr für Jahr um das Zwei- bis Dreifache weil strukturierte Daten automatisch Mehrwert schaffen“. Heute verbindet Covalo über 1’500 Lieferanten mit mehr als 6’000 Marken wie Givaudan, Symrise, PUIG und La Prairie, und arbeitet mit Organisationen wie Reed Exhibitions, TraceOne sowie der B Corp Beauty Coalition zusammen. Im vergangenen Jahr stieg das Engagement auf der Plattform um 84 Prozent mit 1,3 Millionen monatliche Interaktionen, bei einer Net Revenue Retention von über 145 Prozent. Covalo hat sich nun in einer Seed-Erweiterungsrunde 3,5 Millionen Euro unter der Führung von Hi inov sowie mit Beteiligung der bestehenden Investoren HTGF und seed+speed Ventures gesichert. Mit der neuen Finanzierung will Covalo sein Enterprise-Angebot in wichtigen Märkten weiter skalieren. Ausserdem ist geplant, neue branchenspezifische KI-Agenten einzuführen – darunter Conversational Analytics, Datenextraktion und -anreicherung sowie Compliance-Prüfungen – und die Plattform um zentrale Produkt- und Stammdatenfunktionen zu erweitern. Langfristig ist auch eine Expansion in angrenzende Branchen vorgesehen. (press release / LF) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Covalo AG Company profiles on startup.ch Covalo AG Related News Covalo AG 04.12.2024 16:30 Covalo acquires Italy’s leading platform for the cosmetics industry 20.03.2023 15:00 HTGF invests in Personal Care ingredients platform Related tags

title: Lobby raises USD 2.2 million to automate group bookings across the travel industry
article_text: Lobby raises USD 2.2 million to automate group bookings across the travel industry 01.04.2026 Lobby’s AI-driven platform automates the entire process of group bookings to ensure faster response times, more accurate quotes, among others. The proceeds from the funding round will be used to expand beyond the hospitality sector with the Meetings, Incentives, Conferences and Exhibitions (MICE) vertical, experiences and attractions business, cruises and airlines. Group bookings are a growing revenue priority for travel providers, typically generating more per transaction than individual bookings. However, group bookings are complex and slow to process due to manual workflows. Founded in Zürich in 2025, Lobby has built an AI platform that handles the full lifecycle of a group booking request. The company’s solution ensures faster response times and, therefore, higher conversion rates, more accurate quotes and automated follow-ups on unanswered or declined offers. In particular the platform solves one of the most persistent operational challenges: B2B and group bookings that arrive via e-mail, one of the most important sales channels for hotels, MICE industry, means of transportation, as well as experience and activities. Lobby turns incoming e-mail requests into accurate bookings in 30 seconds. Lobby's platform integrates with Gmail, Outlook and existing property management systems. Customers can choose between full automation or a human-in-the-loop model, in which staff review and approve quotes before they are sent. Lobby has raised USD 2.2 million in funding from Founderful. As part of the investment, Pascal Mathis, Co-Founder of the venture capital firm and of GetYourGuide with wide experience in scaling startups, will join Lobby’s Board of Directors. “Lobby is tackling one of the most complex and valuable segments in travel: group bookings” says Pascal Mathis, Co-Founder and Partner of Founderful. “What stood out to us is their ability to move beyond surface-level automation and execute workflows across systems. The travel experience of the founder team helps to build a solution which solves a real problem of the industry.” After successfully closing the investment round, Lobby plans to expand beyond the hospitality sector with the Meetings, Incentives, Conferences and Exhibitions (MICE) vertical, experiences and attractions business, cruises and airlines as its main goals. As part of its expansion, the company is recruiting various new team members. “Having Founderful’s trust gives us even greater strength to take Lobby to the next level: beyond the hospitality sector and into new verticals across the travel industry” explains Romy Abbrederis, CEO and Co-Founder of Lobby. “With our engine, we will help them automate processes that were previously considered too complex and unlock new levels of efficiency and revenue for our customers.” (Press release/AJ) Photo: Lobby founders L-R: Alex Mugrauer, Romy Abbrederis, Muhammad Abdullah 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Lobby AG Company profiles on startup.ch Lobby AG Related News Lobby AG 02.06.2025 17:16 MassChallenge Switzerland presents 2025 cohort Related tags

title: OOMNIUM sichert sich 870'000 Franken über eigene Plattform
article_text: OOMNIUM sichert sich 870'000 Franken über eigene Plattform 01.04.2026 Die Finanzierungsplattform OOMNIUM will verstärkt die Möglichkeiten von KI nutzen und plant zudem, einen digitalen Business-Angel-Club aufzubauen. Um sich die dafür notwendigen frischen Mittel zu sichern, setzte OOMNIUM auf die eigene Plattform. Mit Erfolg: das ursprüngliche Ziel von 600000 Franken wurde deutlich übertroffen. Vor drei Jahren ist OOMNIUM an den Start gegangen. Seit einem Jahr gibt es nebst den öffentlichen Kampagnen auch private auf der Plattform, bei denen gezielt ausgewählte Investorinnen und Investoren angesprochen werden. Dieses Modell hat OOMNIUM nun erfolgreich selbst genutzt: Die Eigenkampagne wurde Mitte März nach 17 Tagen abgeschlossen und lag deutlich über dem ursprünglichen Ziel von 600’000 Franken. «Wir versprechen den Unternehmen, die mit uns Kapital aufnehmen, schnellere und erfolgreichere Finanzierungsrunden. Mit unserer eigenen Runde haben wir bewiesen, dass unsere Methodik funktioniert», so CEO und Co-Founder Leandro Davies. An der Runde beteiligten sich 32 Personen aus den persönlichen Netzwerken des Teams sowie ausgewählte Investorinnen und Investoren der OOMNIUM-Basis. Ausgegeben wurden sowohl traditionell ausgestellte Namensaktien als auch tokenisierte Partizipationsscheine – abhängig von Phase und Höhe des Investments. Die Runde war in drei Phasen aufgeteilt, die jeweils zwischen fünf und sieben Tagen dauerten. Das Mindestticket lag je nach Phase zwischen 10'000 und 45'000 Franken, wobei die effektiven Beträge in vielen Fällen deutlich darüber lagen. Die Mehrheit der Unternehmensanteile und Stimmrechte ist weiterhin bei den Gründungsaktionärinnen und -aktionären. Mit den frischen Mitteln sollen die Prozesse auf der Plattform weiter automatisiert werden, unter anderem durch den Einsatz von künstlicher Intelligenz. Gleichzeitig investiert OOMNIUM in den Aufbau und die Lancierung des digitalen Business-Angel-Clubs «OOMNIUM Circle». «Wir merken, dass viele Next-Gen-Business Angels andere Ansprüche an die Art und Weise haben, wie und in welchem Rahmen sie ihr Geld anlegen. Dem wollen wir mit dem Aufbau des «OOMNIUM Circle» Rechnung tragen.», erklärt Iva Bozovic, CMO bei OOMNIUM. Zudem rechnet OOMNIUM damit, die Profitabilitätsgrenze noch im Laufe des Jahres zu erreichen. Kurzfristig legt das Team den Fokus auf die Umsetzung: Mehr als ein Dutzend Startups sollen noch im ersten Halbjahr über die Plattform Kapital aufnehmen. (press release / LF) Bild: (v.l.n.r) Arijana Walcott (VR), Cécile Simoness (Campaign Lead), Franz D'Huc (Co-Founder, VRP), Rea Eggli (Co-Founder, VR), Leandro Davies (Co-Founder, CEO), Ruben Feurer (COO) und Iva Bozovic (CMO) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about OOMNIUM AG Related News OOMNIUM AG 09.02.2026 16:00 OOMNIUM verdoppelt Investmentvolumen 31.05.2023 16:15 Crowdinvestment trägt im Mai üppige Blüten Related tags

title: DeltaSpark receives CHF 150'000 from Venture Kick
article_text: DeltaSpark receives CHF 150'000 from Venture Kick 31.03.2026 The EPFL spin-off DeltaSpark has obtained CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick to advance a technology that converts CO₂ into stable minerals while producing cheap and clean hydrogen on-site. The cleantech startup aims to help industries reduce emissions while generating valuable byproducts. Record-high CO₂ emissions are driving global warming and increasing pressure on industries to reduce their carbon footprint. Sectors such as cement, waste-to-energy, and heavy manufacturing face particular challenges, as their emissions are difficult to eliminate. Current carbon capture solutions often store CO₂ without creating additional value, limiting their economic attractiveness. EPFL spin-off DeltaSpark created a compact system that converts CO₂ into solid, stable minerals. Using an electrocatalytic process that is up to 100 times faster than other existing methods, the technology simultaneously produces clean hydrogen and oxygen. By turning emissions into useful resources, this approach offers industries a way to reduce carbon output while generating useful chemicals and materials. DeltaSpark’s CO₂-offset subscription aims to be priced below carbon taxes, allowing large emitters, such as cement plants and incinerators, to cut CO₂ liabilities while gaining strategic access to high-value chemicals. The Venture Kick philanthropic initiative provides seed funding to Swiss startups and offers a well-structured entrepreneurial path toward building a business. The CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick will enable DeltaSpark to accelerate the delivery of its paid pilots and support its seed fundraising round in the next months. “Venture Kick is more than just funding. It also provided us with essential coaching that helped us refine our business plan for our target customer segments,” highlights CEO Luc Bondaz. “Through this support, we were able to capture our customers’ needs, sharpen our value proposition, and develop a clear go-to-market strategy.” DeltaSpark was founded by CEO Dr. Luc Bondaz and CTO Dr. Suhas Nuggehalli Sampathkumar in late 2025. Supported by advisors with expertise in scale-up and process engineering, the team combines technical and industrial knowledge to bring carbon conversion solutions to market. (Press release/AJ) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about DeltaSpark AG Company profiles on startup.ch DeltaSpark AG Related News DeltaSpark AG 27.03.2026 11:00 Tech4Regen 2026 unveils new 15 deeptech cohort 11.03.2026 14:51 InnoBooster supports five early-stage startups with CHF 750’000 Principal Investments 2026 The Swiss Venture Capital Report 2026 offers key insights into the latest trends shaping the investment landscape. title: Zürich-based Starmind acquired by US AI platform company Capacity
article_text: Zürich-based Starmind acquired by US AI platform company Capacity 31.03.2026 Swiss AI pioneer Starmind has been acquired by US-based support automation platform Capacity. Starmind's technology, which identifies which employees hold undocumented organizational knowledge, will be integrated into Capacity's platform. For years, companies have invested in documenting knowledge across wikis, intranets, and knowledge bases. With the rise of generative AI, this documented knowledge has become more accessible than ever. Yet a fundamental problem remains: a large share of the most critical knowledge in organizations is never written down, and for AI systems, what is not documented simply does not exist. Founded in 2010, Starmind helps large organizations surface expertise that exists in people's heads but never makes it into documents or databases. Its AI infers who knows what inside a company and routes queries to the right person. The company counts several large corporations among its customers, including Pepsico, Roche and Accenture. “Enterprises don’t fail because they lack documents. They fail because they can’t reach the people who actually know,” said Marc Vontobel, CEO of Starmind. “Starmind has spent 16 years making undocumented knowledge accessible.” Capacity, founded in 2017 and headquartered in the United States, acquired Starmind. Capacity offers an AI-powered support automation platform. More than 20,000 companies including 3M, Disney, American Express and Sony use Capacity for external customer support and internal employee enablement. Capacity has marked significant growth in 2025 and is now profitable with $60 million of annual recurring revenue. The company has raised more than $155 million in both equity and debt. This acquisition extends Capacity's offering beyond documented knowledge to include what the company calls "living, human expertise." “Our mission has always been to help teams do their best work by simplifying how organizations support customers and employees,” said David Karandish, CEO of Capacity. “By bringing Starmind into Capacity, we’re strengthening the knowledge foundation behind every customer and employee interaction.” (Press release / AJ) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Starmind International AG Company profiles on startup.ch Starmind International AG Highlights Starmind International AG Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2014 Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2015 Related News Starmind International AG 27.07.2022 09:00 Startups rely on seasoned board members’ expertise 09.05.2022 08:50 C-Level managers for tech startups Related tags

title: Marco Odermatt investiert in Obwaldner Startup
article_text: Marco Odermatt investiert in Obwaldner Startup 30.03.2026 Wie der Tages-Anzeiger berichtet, steigt Skistar Marco Odermatt neu als Investor bei Gaisbock ein. Damit folgt er dem Beispiel anderer prominenter Spitzensportler wie Dario Cologna, Lara Gut-Behrami oder Wendy Holdener, die sich ebenfalls unternehmerisch engagieren. Marco Odermatt, Olympiasieger und fünfmaliger Gesamtweltcupsieger in Folge, zählt zu den erfolgreichsten Schweizer Skirennfahrern. Nun bringt der Nidwaldner seine Erfahrung aus dem Spitzensport auch in die Wirtschaft ein: Laut Tages-Anzeiger beteiligt er sich am Sarner Startup Gaisbock. Sein Anteil liegt zwischen zehn und zwanzig Prozent. Gaisbock entwickelt natürliche Pflege- und Kosmetikprodukte mit Schwerpunkt auf Männerpflege. Ergänzend bietet das 2023 gegründete Unternehmen Seren für Sportlerinnen und Sportler an, die unter anderem die Regeneration nach körperlicher Belastung unterstützen sollen. Odermatt wird in Zukunft die Produkte testen und Rückmeldung zu deren Wirkung geben. Die Produkte werden in der eigenen Manufaktur in Büron im Luzerner Hinterland entwickelt und produziert. Sie sind im Sportfachhandel sowie in Apotheken, Drogerien und online erhältlich. Geschäftsführer Ramon Riebli und Marco Odermatt kennen sich noch aus ihrer gemeinsamen Zeit an der Sportschule Engelberg – nun arbeiten sie erstmals auch geschäftlich zusammen. Odermatt ist nicht der einzige Spitzensportler, der in Unternehmen investiert. Unter den Olympiasiegerinnen und Olympiasiegern aus der Wintersportwelt finden sich zahlreiche prominente Beispiele: Der Langläufer Dario Cologna ist am Getränke-Startup KAEX beteiligt, ebenso wie Lara Gut-Behrami. Auch die beiden Skirennfahrerinnen Wendy Holdener und Michelle Gisin engagieren sich als Investorinnen beim Sportnahrungs-Startup Nutriathletic. (Press release / LF) Bild: Gaisbock Shampoo / via Gaisbock.ch 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Gaisbock AG Related tags

title: CHF 1 million to Scale Miros Swiss Network of Connected Workpods
article_text: CHF 1 million to Scale Miros Swiss Network of Connected Workpods 30.03.2026 miros, a Lausanne-based startup founded in 2023 out of the EPFL ecosystem, announced the closing of a CHF 1 million Pre-Seed funding round from various business angels supporting the vision. The company will use the funds to deploy a network of connected and bookable workpods across Switzerland, with a target of more than 100 units by the end of 2026. miros is building a network of connected workpods to bring privacy, calm and comfort into public and semi-public spaces. Through its hardware and software platform, including a mobile app to locate, book and access pods, the company addresses the needs of a workforce that is increasingly mobile and flexible. “We start with pods, but the ambition goes far beyond that,” said Dr. Fabio Zuliani, Founder and CEO of miros. “We are looking at how commercial real estate itself can become flexible, modular and on-demand. The pod is just the first building block. Seeing it already used throughout Switzerland and beyond, makes that vision feel very real.” The round follows a manufacturing partnership with Ducommun Menuisiers, initiated through the SyNNergy grant from Innovaud. The pods are designed in collaboration with Lakabi, a Lausanne-based furniture brand, and fully built in Switzerland. This setup brings together engineering, software, design and craftsmanship to scale production while keeping strong attention to detail. In parallel, the project also received support from the Canton of Vaud to develop a new version made entirely from Swiss wood (Bois Suisse), reinforcing its focus on local sourcing and production. Since its inception, miros has deployed 15 workpods and worked with partners including SwissTech Convention Center, Beaulieu, and Hôpital Riviera-Chablais. Through Geneva Airport’s GVA Runway Lab open innovation program, miros was able to test its value proposition in real conditions to refine the oder and confirm strong product-market fit. The company has also taken its first step internationally, with a pod deployed since October at Village by Crédit Agricole in Toulouse, France. These deployments helped validate the product and build the foundations for scalingw with close to 350 hours of usage so far. Neil Chennoufi, Co-Founder and COO of miros, added: “What makes this collaboration special is the connection between people. We know the individuals crafting each pod in the workshop, and we know those installing them on site. It is rare to bridge craftsmanship and startups in such a direct way.” The company plans to announce several new partnerships in the coming months as it continues to expand its footprint across Switzerland. (PR) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about MIROS Technology SA Company profiles on startup.ch MIROS Technology SA Related News MIROS Technology SA 07.05.2025 17:00 La vision de MIROS séduit le programme SyNNergy Principal Investments 2026 The Swiss Venture Capital Report 2026 offers key insights into the latest trends shaping the investment landscape. title: EyeFitU to be acquired by MySize subsidiary Naiz Fit
article_text: EyeFitU to be acquired by MySize subsidiary Naiz Fit 30.03.2026 Fashion-tech company MySize plans to acquire several assets from Zurich-based sizing technology startup EyeFitU through its subsidiary Naiz Fit. The deal would transfer EyeFitU's proprietary sizing algorithms, brand partnerships and platform technology into the Naiz Fit platform. EyeFitU’s AI-based SizeEngine matches brand size charts to individual consumers' body measurements and fit preferences, providing size recommendations for garments, shoes and accessories. The platform combines brand and retailer data with user feedback to refine its algorithm over time. Founded in 2013, the company has grown to approximately CHF 350,000 in annual recurring revenue and counts HEAD, Paul & Shark, POC, Giant, Assos, Ecoalf, Odlo and CMP among its brand partners. This track record has attracted the attention of MySize, a Nasdaq-listed fashion-tech company built around four businesses that tackle two expensive problems in online fashion: shoppers buying the wrong size and brands getting stuck with too much inventory. MySize's platform includes Naiz Fit for apparel and footwear sizing, Percentil for secondhand, Orgad for overstock, and 10peaks for regional brand distribution. Naiz Fit has signed a non-binding Letter of Intent to acquire selected assets of EyeFitU. "EyeFitU has developed strong technology and valuable relationships with international brands," said Ronen Luzon, Founder, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of MySize. "Integrating these assets into our Naiz Fit platform strengthens our technology base and expands our reach across key European and international markets. We expect this revenue to be accretive to Naiz Fit and believe it has the potential to grow as we leverage EyeFitU's brand relationships and technology within our broader platform." The proposed acquisition includes EyeFitU's proprietary sizing algorithms, platform technology and codebase, Shopify integration APIs, FootScanner application, anonymized sizing databases, trademarks, domain portfolio, customer contracts, and CRM data. Upon closing, these assets are expected to be integrated into the Naiz Fit platform to enhance its AI-powered sizing capabilities and expand its global customer base. EyeFitU founder and CEO Isabelle Ohnemus and CTO Erik Troelsen are expected to support the transition and integration process following the execution of definitive agreements. (Press release/AJ) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about EYEFITU AG Company profiles on startup.ch EYEFITU AG Related News EYEFITU AG 26.07.2021 15:53 EyeFitU gains momentum with its fashion-tech SaaS platform 29.03.2018 09:58 Two Swiss winners at the Fashion Innovation Award Related tags

title: Plair attracts investment from bioMérieux
article_text: Plair attracts investment from bioMérieux 27.03.2026 bioMérieux, a global leader in the field of in vitro diagnostics, has invested more than CHF 1 million in Plair, a scale-up company offering a breakthrough solution for real-time microbial detection and air sampling. PLAIR sees this partnership as a major endorsement of its flagship innovation, RAPID-C+, and a decisive step toward setting a new global standard for cleanroom monitoring. Plair’s flagship innovation, RAPID-C+, is a unique real-time viable particle counter that sets a new industry standard in pharmaceutical environmental monitoring. RAPID-C+ is already commercially available. This partnership reinforces bioMérieux’s commitment to driving innovation in environmental monitoring and supporting the evolving needs of the pharmaceutical industry. RAPID-C+ combines real-time counting of viable and total particles with certified continuous sampling on standard agar media, enabling microbial identification and supporting faster, more-informed decision making in cleanroom environments. As part of this collaboration, bioMérieux Senior Vice President of Pharma Quality Control Michael Reynier will join Plair’s Board of Directors. “Our investment in Plair is a strategic step forward in our ambition to be the partner of choice for advancing pharmaceutical quality control,” said Reynier. “RAPID-C+ is a breakthrough innovation that complements our portfolio and supports our customers in delivering critical therapies quickly and safely to patients.” Geneva-based PLAIR will continue working closely with its global ecosystem of partners and distributors to bring RAPID-C+ to pharmaceutical manufacturers worldwide. “An investment from a global leader like bioMérieux brings unique synergies to Plair, creating significant value for all our stakeholders, partners, and RAPID-C+ users,” said Plair CEO Denis Kiselev. “The acceleration of the bio-fluorescent particle counter market demands that quality managers and microbiologists receive clear regulatory support and scientific guidance to adopt this new method. We believe this endorsement by bioMérieux will become a strong signal to the industry, ultimately resulting in better, safer medicines for patients.” This investment reflects bioMérieux’s strategy to diversify and strengthen the Pharma Quality Control offering, which is built on a partner-first approach and over 60 years of scientific excellence. With a global presence in more than 160 countries and a team dedicated to innovation, bioMérieux is recognized as a trusted reference by pharmaceutical leaders worldwide. The family-owned company is headquartered in Marcy-l'Étoile, France, close to Lyon. (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Plair SA Company profiles on startup.ch Plair SA Highlights Plair SA Winner Venture Kick Related News Plair SA 05.09.2025 14:20 Crowd-Support für weiteres Wachstum 08.07.2025 09:06 Grössere Brötchen backen mit Geld von der Crowd Related tags

title: Solar Monkey and Eturnity merge and attract fresh capital
article_text: Solar Monkey and Eturnity merge and attract fresh capital 27.03.2026 Eturnity and Solar Monkey merge to form the European leader in installer software. The new company is backed with fresh capital by a group of European investors. Together, Solar Monkey, founded in 2015 in the Netherlands, and Swiss-based Eturnity deliver software to more than 2,000 installers across 8+ European countries. Their software provides the backbone for the full installer process, from design and sales to installation and permitting. This enables installers to tackle the complexities of today’s energy market and improve their cost competitiveness. The merger enables the combination of Solar Monkey’s software for intuitive design and sales with Eturnity’s strength in end-to-end workflows and complex project engineering. The full range of energy installations and related products will be supported. With a footprint across 8 European countries, the new entity can provide superior economies of scale and emerges as the leading installer software in Europe. New investment The new company is backed with fresh capital by investors including Klima (Spain), Junction (Belgium), Eneco Ventures (Netherlands) and Helen (Finland). As part of a capital increase, Eturnity AG has acquired shares in Solar Monkey B.V. and issued new registered shares in return. Furthermore, Solar Monkey founder and CEO Jan Pieter Versluijs and Anusha Nirmalananthan, a non-executive director at Solar Monkey, have joined the Board of Directors of Eturnity AG. Both brands and products continue to operate in the foreseeable future. At the same time, a stronger and more comprehensive range of software products will be developed. The market is rapidly expanding beyond solar panels toward complete home electrification systems. Installers are moving from single-product solar projects to integrated energy systems including products like batteries and heat pumps. This drastically increases the complexity of the installers’ jobs. At the same time, the pressure on labour costs (which accounts to 60% of the total cost) is mounting. “Basically, all installers need to grasp this increasing complexity”, says Matthias Wiget, CEO of Eturnity. “Installers can no longer compete without a strong software backbone to streamline their increasingly complicated work. From lead to installation, every step in the workflow needs to be digitised. That’s where the biggest gains are.” The founders of both companies have known each other for years through the European solar industry. “We always shared respect for what the other team was building,” says Jan Pieter Versluijs, founder of Solar Monkey. “By joining forces, we combine deep market knowledge and complementary strengths across markets and products. That allows us to innovate faster and build even better software for installers across Europe.” (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Eturnity AG Company profiles on startup.ch Eturnity AG Highlights Eturnity AG Innosuisse Certificate Winner Venture Kick Related News Eturnity AG 07.06.2023 10:00 Eturnity: Acht Millionen Franken für weiteres Wachstum 15.05.2023 16:30 Swissnex San Francisco reveals first cohorts for its sector-based bootcamps Related tags

title: UK patient data platform Sanius Health acquires Kielo Research
article_text: UK patient data platform Sanius Health acquires Kielo Research 27.03.2026 Sanius Health, a leading real-world evidence and patient-generated data company focused on rare and underserved conditions, has acquired Zug-based Kielo Research. Founded in 2022, the company focusses on integrating the patient perspective into the development and evaluation of medicines, medical technologies and healthcare services. Kielo Research is recognised for its deep expertise in patient preference studies, patient-reported outcomes and qualitative research that captures how patients experience diseases and treatments. These insights enable healthcare organisations to design more effective therapies, clinical trials and care pathways by ensuring patient perspectives are meaningfully incorporated throughout the development process. By combining Kielo’s expertise in patient-centred research with Sanius Health’s secure research infrastructure, advanced analytics and patient engagement capabilities, Sanius will expand its ability to generate high-quality evidence that reflects real patient experiences and priorities. It also expands Sanius’ expertise across a broad set of therapeutic areas, including cardiovascular disease, oncology, respiratory medicine, metabolic disease, neurology, dermatology and vaccine development. Sanius Health operates across the United States, the United Kingdom and Asia, working with healthcare providers, patients, researchers and life sciences companies to support better care and the development of treatments through the responsible use of health insights, clinical expertise and advanced analytics. Through its rare disease and oncology registries, Sanius Health supports clinical trial recruitment, regulatory validation and reimbursement pathways, connecting healthcare providers, researchers and life sciences organisations through established research partnerships. Combined with integration across one of the largest primary care provider footprints in the United Kingdom, the platform supports patient management, care delivery, pathway redesign, cost improvement and accelerated clinical trials, while generating decision-grade evidence for regulators, payers and life sciences partners. The platform holds particular strength in rare diseases and haematology, with programmes across sickle cell disease (SCD), myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs), multiple myeloma (MM), Waldenström’s macroglobulinaemia (WM), chronic myeloid leukaemia (CML) and chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (CLL). Tommi Tervonen, Chief Scientist & Founder of Kielo Research, comments: “We are delighted to join Sanius Health. Both organisations share a strong commitment to ensuring that the patient voice is heard and reflected in the development of new treatments and healthcare solutions. By combining Kielo’s expertise in patient-centred research with Sanius Health’s technology and global research capabilities, we will generate deeper insights into patient experiences and preferences.” (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Kielo Research GmbH Related tags

title: 1.8M Seed Round to fuel SolidWatts Industrial Process Heating Vision
article_text: 1.8M Seed Round to fuel SolidWatts Industrial Process Heating Vision 26.03.2026 SolidWatts announces the closure of CHF 1.8 million Seed funding to accelerate the development and market entry of its innovative industrial process heating technology. This seed funding will be used to scale SolidWatts’ platform to higher power levels and to conduct pilots with industrial partners and clients actively seeking to reduce their energy consumption and expenditure by electrifying heating processes. Pully-based SolidWatts is on a mission to revolutionize industrial heating. Its proprietary, cutting-edge solid-state Radio Frequency (RF) platform delivers high power dielectric heating at efficiencies and scale that make it a direct replacement for traditional fossil fuel-based processes - for the first time. With a focus on sustainability and energy efficiency, and with support from the Swiss Climate Foundation, the company is dedicated to helping power-hungry industries transition to clean, electrified, solutions. Applicable to traditional industries and emerging ones, SolidWatts’ technology enables energy-intensive industries, such as chemical and pharmaceutical, food processing, carbon capture and advanced recycling. SolidWatts’ platform enables these sectors to electrify their processes, cutting both energy consumption and CO₂ emissions, improving overall efficiency. Much like a domestic microwave oven, SolidWatts’ technology creates electromagnetic waves to heat materials from the inside - a process known as dielectric heating. Unlike traditional heating methods using boilers, ovens and furnaces, SolidWatts’ technology is highly efficient, directly converting 80% or more of the electricity into RF heating power, with minimal energy lost to surrounding infrastructure. And because the system is solid-state, maintenance and service requirements are low and uptime is high. The round saw continued commitment from existing investors Evercurious VC, Kickfund and Axel Carbon Capital, while welcoming a new cohort of backers including Uni.Fund, Investing for Purpose, Loggerhead Ventures, Almanac Ventures and a group of angel investors. “This investment is a tremendous endorsement of our mission to transform how industry consumes energy and allows us to bring RF technology to the power levels and efficiencies that industry actually needs,” said Dr. Markus Aicheler, Founder and CTO of SolidWatts. “Industrial process heating accounts for around 20% of global energy demand and roughly 10% of worldwide CO₂ emissions - yet it remains one of the hardest sectors to decarbonise. As global energy demand rises alongside the AI boom, improving efficiency becomes essential.” Electrifying even 1% of these processes with SolidWatts’ technology could eliminate 36 million tonnes of CO₂ each year - the equivalent of removing 7.8 million cars from the road. SolidWatts was founded in November 2022. Five years ago, when geopolitical tensions exposed the fragility of global energy supply, Dr. Aicheler - a former post-doctoral Fellow at CERN - drew inspiration from CERN’s pioneering use of high-power solid-state RF technology. While CERN leverages this technology for scientific applications, Dr. Aicheler saw the potential for high-power RF to displace fossil fuels in industry. Dr. George Georgiadis, Partner at Evercurious VC, representing the investment group: “SolidWatts powers the future of industrial resilience. Their technology drives efficiency and provides a clear path away from fossil fuel dependency. This investment reflects our mission to back European hard-tech champions that solve real-world problems. We are scaling an innovator born in Europe and built for the world.” (PR) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about SolidWatts SA Company profiles on startup.ch SolidWatts SA Highlights SolidWatts SA Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2024 Winner Venture Kick Related News SolidWatts SA 29.01.2026 09:00 LeadiNNg to Scale-up set to propel promising 5th cohort 20.01.2026 17:15 ZKB Pionierpreis Technopark presents shortlist Related tags

title: Coteries s'unit à Idéative
article_text: Coteries s'unit à Idéative 26.03.2026 Idéative annonce l’intégration de l’agence Coteries SA, renforçant ainsi son pôle de compétences digitales. Cette étape importante du développement de l’entreprise s’accompagne de l’ouverture d’une succursale à Bienne. Par cette expansion géographique, Idéative consolide son ancrage dans le Mittelland. L’union de Coteries et d’Idéative permet de proposer un spectre de prestations unique sur le marché suisse. Le savoir-faire reconnu de Coteries dans la réalisation de sites internet sur le CMS Webflow vient compléter l’expertise historique d’Idéative sur les technologies open source TYPO3 et Drupal. La capacité de développement d’applications sur mesure se trouve renforcée par cette intégration. « En combinant nos forces, nous offrons désormais une réponse technologique sur mesure pour chaque projet, qu’il s’agisse de solutions légères et rapides ou d’écosystèmes digitaux complexes », précise Christophe Miville, directeur d’Idéative. Outre le volet technique, Idéative renforce son pôle créatif avec de nouvelles compétences de pointe en design et UX/UI, ainsi qu’en développement web. Une équipe d’experts pour assurer la continuité L'équipe de Coteries a rejoint les rangs d'Idéative au début de l'année 2026. Afin de garantir une transition fluide et la pérennité des collaborations en cours, les visages de l'agence restent aux commandes : Sébastien Flury, Directeur Adrien Cominotto, Senior Lead Developer Sébastien Von Roth & Pep Borras, Senior UX/UI Designers Thandi Fehlmann , Social Media Manager Expansion vers le Mittelland Ce rapprochement s'accompagne d'une ambition géographique forte: l'ouverture d'une succursale Idéative à Bienne. Stratégiquement située au cœur du Mittelland, cette antenne rayonne sur les cantons de Berne, Fribourg, Jura, Neuchâtel et Soleure. La direction de cette nouvelle entité est confiée à Sébastien Flury. Habitant du Jura bernois et figure reconnue de l'écosystème entrepreneurial national, il a pour mission de développer l’empreinte de l’agence dans cette région tout en renforçant la proximité avec les clients historiques. Idéative et Coteries collaborent avec des entreprises de l’Arc jurassien dans les domaines de l’horlogerie, du luxe et de l’industrie (Posalux, Monwitt), l’Institut Fédéral de la Propriété Intellectuelle (IPI), l’Office fédéral du sport (OFSPO), le Swiss Center for Design and Health (SCDH) ou encore la Fédération Suisse des Médecins (FMH). (PR) Sur la photo: De g. à dr. Sébastien Von Roth, Sébastien Flury, Christophe Miville, Josep Borras, Adrien Cominotto, Fabrice Léger, crédit : GPPA/Sébastien Moritz 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Coteries SA Company profiles on startup.ch Coteries SA Related News Coteries SA 04.04.2022 15:48 New Milestone Cedille German model released 09.11.2021 13:50 Swiss startup launches milestone French language model Related tags

title: MESH secures US$3.8 million seed round to automate rebar construction globally
article_text: MESH secures US$3.8 million seed round to automate rebar construction globally 26.03.2026 MESH, an ETH Zurich spin-off, closed an oversubscribed US$3.8 million seed funding round backed by industry leaders and experienced investors including ABB Robotics Ventures and Sika. This investment round marks a pivotal milestone for MESH as it transitions from a regional innovator to a global technology provider. While construction is planned with sophisticated digital tools, the actual construction work remains heavily manual and physically demanding. To overcome this challenge, MESH has developed a software and robotics platform specifically for the rebar industry. MESH’s easy-to-use software allows construction companies to deploy industrial robots to handle, place, and join rebar autonomously. Unlike traditional factory robots designed for repetitive tasks, MESH’s system is highly flexible. Robots can switch between different designs at the push of a button without requiring specialized programming, enabling highly flexible automated rebar production. Proven in the Field Founded in 2022 and based in the canton Aargau, MESH has rapidly transitioned its technology from the ETH Zurich research lab into practical industrial applications. Its systems are already operating in Swiss rebar shops and prefabrication plants to automate high-stakes construction work. To date, more than 1 million rebar elements have been processed by MESH systems for major infrastructure projects, including Switzerland’s new Gotthard tunnel and building construction works. These deployments have proven that automation does more than just speed up the process. It makes rebar work safer by removing workers from dangerous, repetitive tasks and by significantly reducing errors through built-in digital quality control. Strategic Investment The investment round was backed by ABB Robotics Ventures, Apprecia Capital, Shimizu Corporation - one of Japan’s leading general contractors -, specialty chemicals giant Sika, buildify.earth and Aargauische Kantonalbank. By partnering with industry leaders such as ABB Robotics, Shimizu Corporation, and Sika, MESH is gaining the strategic expertise and international reach critical to bringing its automated solutions to rebar construction worldwide. The company will use the funding to accelerate international expansion and establish a strong presence in key global markets. In addition, the capital will support the development of new partnership models designed to help rebar and construction firms easily adopt MESH technology, while supporting team expansion. Marc Segura, President of ABB Robotics, comments: “We are delighted to be part of this transformative ecosystem and to support MESH in advancing automation for the construction industry. As the sector addresses labor shortages, safety challenges and the need for greater productivity, automating rebar production offers a clear path for delivering faster fabrication, consistent quality and reduced material waste. Combining ABB Robotics’ expertise with MESH’s platform will bridge the gap between digital design and physical execution in construction projects and enable safer, more e5icient and reliable rebar production.” Ammar Mirjan, CEO and Co-Founder of MESH, adds: “Securing the support of our partners and global leaders in robotics and construction is a pivotal milestone for MESH. Our vision is to bring the efficiency of the digital world to physical construction, and this investment is a major step toward that goal. With this partnership, we are ready to scale our technology and make a lasting impact on how the world builds." (Press release / SK) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Mesh AG Company profiles on startup.ch Mesh AG Related News Mesh AG 29.07.2022 12:40 PERI und Sika beteiligen sich am ETH Spinoff MESH Related tags

title: SEALSQ to acquire Miraex
article_text: SEALSQ to acquire Miraex 25.03.2026 SEALSQ Corp, a subsidiary of WISeKey International has signed a Letter of Intent (“LOI”) to acquire 100% of the equity interest of Miraex SA, a developer of photonics-based quantum interconnect solutions that bridge quantum processors and quantum networks. The LOI provides for a 60-day exclusivity period during which the parties will conduct confirmatory due diligence and negotiate definitive transaction documents and is binding on the part of Miraex. The Miraex acquisition is expected to be finalized by the end of June 2026, subject to the completion of customary closing conditions and regulatory processes applicable to acquisitions of this nature. The transaction would be carried out using the SEALSQ Quantum Fund as part of SEALSQ’s strategic investment program to build a fully integrated Quantum Vertical Stack. Connecting Quantum Resources Miraex is a developer of photonics-based quantum interconnect solutions, a photonics-based technology layer that enables quantum information to be converted, transmitted, and linked between separated quantum processors and future quantum networks. Based at EPFL Innovation Park, Miraex is one of the very few companies globally working to unlock the full potential of quantum technologies through a Photonic Integrated Circuit (PIC) platform based on Thin Film Lithium Tantalate (TFLT) technology. Miraex operates across three critical quantum verticals: Distributed Quantum Computing (DQS) – enabling the interconnection of distributed quantum processors to achieve scalable quantum computational power. Quantum Sensing – delivering distributed quantum sensing solutions based on photonic entanglement for applications in navigation, geophysics, and defense. Quantum Networking – building globally entangled quantum networks that form the backbone of the future quantum internet. Miraex’s patent portfolio covers direct electro-optical microwave-optical and optical-microwave transduction schemes, as well as traveling wave transducer designs, delivering ultra-high RF-to-optical conversion efficiency. Miraex is also capable of generating photon entanglement across microwave and THz frequency domains separated by a factor of 10,000, a breakthrough capability with profound implications for secure quantum communications and distributed quantum processing. Completing the SEALSQ Quantum Vertical Stack SEALSQ’s vision for its Quantum Vertical Stack encompasses the full spectrum of quantum technology, from quantum-secure semiconductors and post-quantum cryptography (PQC) chips to quantum hardware, and quantum interconnectivity. Miraex’s quantum interconnect platform addresses a pivotal layer of this stack: Quantum Interconnect Layer: Miraex’s technology enables the conversion between microwave (the operating domain of superconducting quantum processors) and optical frequencies (the medium of choice for quantum communication), thereby bridging quantum computing hardware with quantum networks, a foundational requirement for any distributed quantum architecture. Chip-Level Quantum Photonics: Miraex’s compact TFLT PIC designs provide OEM-ready photonic components that can be integrated into SEALSQ’s semiconductor roadmap, enabling next-generation quantum-safe silicon solutions. Distributed Quantum Sensing for Critical Infrastructure: Miraex’s DQS solutions extend SEALSQ’s offering into the domain of ultra-precise quantum sensing for aerospace, defense, financial services, and critical infrastructure protection. With Miraex integrated into SEALSQ, the Company aims to expand and strengthen its vertically integrated quantum technology stack Reinforcing the SEALSQ Quantum Spatial Orbital Cloud The acquisition of Miraex, once completed, should be a decisive accelerant for the SEALSQ Quantum Space Commercial Quantum Spatial Orbital Cloud (QSOC) initiative, SEALSQ’s flagship program to deploy quantum-secure capabilities across Low Earth Orbit (LEO) and beyond. Miraex’s technology is designed to be uniquely aligned with the requirements of a space-based quantum infrastructure. In particular, Space-Grade Photonic Integration is enabled by TFLT PICs, which are compact, power-efficient, and radiation-tolerant by nature, critical attributes for satellite payloads. Their small form factor enables deployment on nanosatellites and CubeSats within the QSOC constellation. Miraex’s photonics-based technologies support the vision of next-generation quantum infrastructure, including global quantum networking and advanced sensing capabilities. These capabilities align with future space-based architectures by enabling the connection of distributed systems and high-precision sensing applications. Together, they represent key building blocks for the development of scalable quantum networks and services across terrestrial and orbital environments. Daniel Brau, CEO of Miraex, commented, “We are excited about the signing of this LOI and the opportunity to join SEALSQ through the Quantum Fund. We are proud of what we have achieved. Our TFLN PIC platform has been designed from the ground up to be the connective tissue of the quantum ecosystem, linking quantum processors, quantum sensors, and quantum networks into a unified infrastructure. We believe SEALSQ’s global reach, semiconductor expertise, and QSOC vision will accelerate our mission to connect quantum resources at planetary scale.” (PR) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about Miraex SA Company profiles on startup.ch Miraex SA Highlights Miraex SA Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2020 Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2021 Top 100 Swiss Startup Award 2022 Winner Venture Kick Related News Miraex SA 28.03.2025 11:00 Startup Camp Korea welcomes five startups 06.02.2025 13:30 Ten Swiss tech startups selected for Silicon Valley 2025 roadshow Related tags

title: PAVE Space raises $40M to disrupt satellite deployment
article_text: PAVE Space raises $40M to disrupt satellite deployment 25.03.2026 PAVE Space, a Swiss space infrastructure company, today announced it has raised USD 40 million in seed funding to develop a new generation of spacecraft designed to move satellites rapidly between orbits. The new funding will help PAVE Space move from development into real-world testing and early commercial operations. The investment ranks among the largest seed financings in the global space sector in recent years. Lausanne-based PAVE Space is building a family of orbital transfer vehicles (OTVs) capable of transporting satellites from low Earth orbit (LEO) to higher-energy destinations such as geostationary orbit (GEO), medium Earth orbit (MEO), or lunar trajectories in less than 24 hours — addressing one of the emerging logistical bottlenecks of the rapidly expanding space economy. Today, most rockets deliver satellites only to low Earth orbit. From there, spacecraft must rely on their own electric propulsion systems to slowly climb to their operational orbit, a process that can take six to twelve months and delay revenue-generating services while tying up capital. With more than 12,000 satellites already in orbit and thousands more launched each year, demand for rapid and flexible mobility between orbits is growing quickly. PAVE Space is building the infrastructure to enable it. Solving the logistics bottleneck in orbit PAVE Space's orbital transfer vehicles will move satellites from low Earth orbit to their final operational orbit in less than 24 hours. Its flagship system, a heavy kickstage vehicle, delivers them to higher-energy orbits quickly and reliably. By replacing the traditional electric orbit-raising process, the platform significantly reduces mission timelines while lowering overall mission cost. PAVE's architecture uses storable bipropellants rather than cryogenic fuels, eliminating boil-off constraints inside the fairing, and supporting long-duration missions that cryogenic systems cannot serve. For satellite operators, the impact is substantial. Services can be brought online months earlier, avoiding the inefficiencies and opportunity costs associated with slow orbital transfer. Beyond commercial missions, this capability is also becoming increasingly critical for defense and institutional customers, who require rapid and flexible mobility in orbit. Alongside the heavy kickstage system, PAVE is also developing a smaller mobile platform designed for responsive missions and dual-use applications, enabling satellites and payloads to move rapidly between orbital positions. A new generation of European space engineers PAVE Space was founded in Lausanne in 2024 by Julie Böhning (CEO) and Jérémy Marciacq (CTO), who have been building rockets together since their student years at EPFL. The pair previously co-founded the Gruyère Space Program, the first European reusable rocket initiative. With just CHF 250,000 in sponsorship funding, the team designed and built a reusable rocket demonstrator that completed 53 test flights in 2024. That experience shaped PAVE’s engineering philosophy: maintain full control over the vehicle stack and iterate rapidly through real-world testing. Today the company develops propulsion systems, avionics, control algorithms and structural design internally, enabling rapid engineering cycles and tight integration across the platform. During the past four months, the team has designed, built and tested the propulsion system for its first mobile platform. The company also recently opened a dedicated propulsion test facility — a former Alpine power plant converted into a rocket engine test site — where PAVE will conduct its first rocket engine firings later this year. In parallel, the company is preparing its first in-space demonstration mission scheduled for October. Commercial demand is also building rapidly. The company has already secured 8 reservation agreements with satellite operators and manufacturers and is engaged in discussions with several major industry players. Scaling Europe’s orbital logistics infrastructure The funding round was led by Visionaries Club and Creandum, with participation from Lombard Odier Investment Managers, Atlantic Labs, Sistafund, b2venture, ACE Investment Partners, Ilavaska Vuillermoz Capital, Pareto & Motier Ventures. It ranks among the largest seed financings in the global space sector in recent years. The new funding will allow PAVE Space to: accelerate development of its heavy kickstage logistics platform execute its first in-orbit technical demonstration mission expand its engineering and operations teams prepare its first commercial orbital logistics missions conduct the first ground firing of its propulsion system With a rapidly growing team of engineers and space industry specialists, PAVE is now hiring across propulsion, avionics, mission design and business roles. Building the logistics backbone of the space economy “The space economy is entering an industrial phase where logistics will become as critical in orbit as they are on Earth,” said Julie Böhning, CEO and Co-Founder of PAVE Space. “Our ambition is to build the infrastructure that allows industries to move, operate and scale beyond Earth - while ensuring Europe retains sovereign capabilities in this next strategic domain.” Investors see PAVE as a key infrastructure player emerging at the intersection of several powerful trends: sovereign European space access, accelerating defence investment and the rise of a global space logistics economy. “Getting to space has become routine but moving once you’re there has not.” said Marton Sarkadi Nagy, Partner at Visionaries Club. “PAVE is building the logistics platform that will enable the next generation of commercial and industrial missions in space.” A strategic capability for Europe’s sovereign space ambitions PAVE’s development comes at a time when Europe is placing renewed emphasis on sovereign capabilities in space and beyond. Governments across the continent are investing heavily in space and defense programmes, with more than €55 billion allocated to European defense spending in the coming years and major initiatives underway to strengthen Europe’s autonomy in critical infrastructure sectors. Within this context, orbital mobility is emerging as a strategic capability. Today, Europe lacks an independent logistics provider capable of enabling mobility and responsiveness in and between orbits. PAVE aims to fill that gap. The company’s logistics vehicles are designed to operate with multiple launch providers - including Falcon 9, Ariane 6 and the Japanese MHI - providing European institutions and satellite operators with a flexible and launcher-agnostic mobility layer in space. This approach allows satellite manufacturers, telecom operators and defence organisations to reach strategic orbits faster and more efficiently while reducing dependency on foreign systems. (PR - ES) Photo: Julie Böhning (CEO) and Jérémy Marciacq (CTO) by Héloïse Maret - Le Nouvelliste 0 Comments FINANCING More news about PAVE Space SA Company profiles on startup.ch PAVE Space SA Related News PAVE Space SA 24.11.2025 11:40 Swiss startups across industries in the global spotlight 07.11.2025 15:45 Swisstech with 26 startups take active role at SLUSH 2025 Related tags

title: NanoZymeX receives CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick
article_text: NanoZymeX receives CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick 25.03.2026 The biotech startup NanoZymeX has obtained CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick to advance a new platform for enzyme replacement therapy using lipid nanoparticles. The technology aims to improve treatment options for rare genetic conditions by increasing therapeutic activity in affected tissues. Enzyme replacement therapies are used to treat rare genetic disorders, but their effectiveness is often limited by poor delivery to the target tissue. In addition, immune responses to repeated treatments can limit long-term effectiveness. NanoZymeX is developing a lipid nanoparticle-based platform designed to transport therapeutic enzymes directly into target cells and lysosomes. By improving intracellular delivery and reducing immune reactions, the approach increases functional activity where it is most needed. Early preclinical studies show strong delivery efficiency and improved enzyme activity in relevant tissues. NanoZymeX’s technology targets lysosomal storage disorders, a group of rare diseases representing a multibillion-dollar therapeutic market, such as Pompe disease, an inherited lysosomal disorder. The University of Basel spin-off is led by Boris Sevarika, Margarita C. Dinamarca, and Eva Hemmrich, who each bring complementary expertise in formulation development, neurobiology, and regulatory science. “The support from Venture Kick comes at a critical moment when scientific discoveries need to transition into real companies,” highlighted Boris Sevarika, co-founder of NanoZymeX. “This type of early funding provides the flexibility needed for company building, business development, and preparing the next stages of financing. It fills a crucial gap between academic research and venture-backed biotech development.” The Venture Kick philanthropic initiative provides seed funding to Swiss startups and offers a well-structured entrepreneurial path toward building a business. The CHF 150,000 from Venture Kick will help NanoZymeX further develop its lipid nanoparticle technology, conduct additional preclinical studies, and prepare scalable manufacturing. This will support future fundraising and the transition toward clinical trials. (Press release/AJ) 0 Comments FINANCING More news about NanoZymeX Related News NanoZymeX 18.03.2026 10:45 Ten Swiss biotech startups selected for Boston roadshow 04.03.2026 14:00 Four startups secure prizes and support Principal Investments 2026 The Swiss Venture Capital Report 2026 offers key insights into the latest trends shaping the investment landscape.
